title: crataegus monogyna subsp. leiomonogyna
intro: Crataegus monogyna subsp. leiomonogyna is a subspecies of the hawthorn tree, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its thorny branches and showy white flowers. The medicinal properties of Crataegus monogyna subsp. leiomonogyna lie in its ability to support cardiovascular health, with flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) contributing to its potential benefits. The health benefits of this plant include lowering blood pressure, improving lipid profiles, and reducing inflammation, making it a popular choice in traditional herbal medicine. Crataegus monogyna subsp. leiomonogyna can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
